label: Ātman
definition: The individual self or soul; pure consciousness underlying personal identity.

label: Brahman
definition: The ultimate reality, cosmic ground of all being; absolute consciousness.

label: Māyā
definition: Cosmic illusion or creative power that causes the appearance of multiplicity.

label: Mokṣa
definition: Liberation from the cycle of birth and death (saṃsāra); the highest puruṣārtha.

label: Karma
definition: Law of action and consequence; accumulated moral causation across lives.

label: Dharma
definition: Moral duty, cosmic order, righteous conduct; varies by context and role.

label: Ahiṃsā
definition: Non-violence; the highest ethical duty in most Hindu schools.

label: Satya
definition: Truthfulness; one of the cardinal virtues across all darśanas.

label: Asteya
definition: Non-stealing; refraining from taking what is not given.

label: Brahmacarya
definition: Celibacy and self-restraint; one of the five yamas in Yoga.

label: Aparigraha
definition: Non-covetousness; non-possessiveness toward material things.

label: Saṃsāra
definition: The cycle of birth, death, and rebirth driven by karma.

label: Jīva
definition: The individual living self; distinct from ātman in Dvaita; identical in Advaita.

label: Īśvara
definition: The personal God; creator, sustainer, and dissolver of the universe.

label: Prakṛti
definition: Primordial matter/nature; material substrate of the universe (Sāṃkhya).

label: Puruṣa
definition: Pure consciousness; the witness-self distinct from Prakṛti in Sāṃkhya.

label: Avidyā
definition: Ignorance; the root cause of saṃsāra in Advaita Vedānta.

label: Vidyā
definition: Knowledge; especially liberating knowledge of brahman/ātman identity.

label: Pratyakṣa
definition: Perception; direct sensory knowledge. Valid pramāṇa in all schools.

label: Anumāna
definition: Inference; logical deduction. Central pramāṇa in Nyāya.

label: Śabda
definition: Testimony; valid knowledge from reliable authority, especially Vedas.

label: Upamāna
definition: Comparison/analogy; knowing the unknown through similarity to the known.

label: Arthāpatti
definition: Postulation; inferring an unperceived fact to explain a perceived one.

label: Nirvāṇa
definition: Extinguishing of ego and desire; liberation concept shared with Buddhism.

label: Turīya
definition: The fourth state of consciousness: pure awareness beyond waking, dream, deep sleep.

label: Vivartavāda
definition: Appearance/illusion theory of causation; Advaita holds the world is apparent transformation.

label: Pariṇāmavāda
definition: Real transformation theory; the world is a genuine transformation of brahman (Rāmānuja).

label: Bhakti
definition: Devotion to a personal deity; primary path to liberation in Bhakti schools.

label: Jñāna
definition: Liberating wisdom/knowledge; primary mokṣa path in Advaita.

label: Karma Yoga
definition: Path of selfless action without attachment to fruits; taught in Bhagavad Gītā.

label: Rāja Yoga
definition: Eight-limbed (aṣṭāṅga) path of meditation and mental discipline (Patañjali).

label: Niṣkāma Karma
definition: Desireless action; performing duty without attachment to results.

label: Varṇāśrama
definition: Social system of four varṇas and four āśramas defining contextual dharma.

label: Pramāṇa
definition: Valid means of knowledge. Nyāya accepts 4; Advaita accepts 6; Mīmāṃsā emphasizes śabda.

label: Loka
definition: Planes of existence (worlds) in Hindu cosmology.

label: Ṛta
definition: Cosmic order and truth in Vedic thought; precursor to dharma.

label: Tapas
definition: Austerity and disciplined effort; purifies and generates spiritual power.

label: Śraddhā
definition: Faith; sincere trust in the guru, scripture, and practice.

label: Ahaṃkāra
definition: Ego-sense; the false identification of ātman with the body-mind complex.

label: Citta
definition: Mind-stuff; the repository of impressions (saṃskāras) in Yoga.

label: Saṃskāra
definition: Mental impressions from past experiences; shape future tendencies.

label: Vāsanā
definition: Latent tendencies/desires arising from saṃskāras; drive saṃsāric existence.

label: Guṇas
definition: Three qualities of Prakṛti: sattva (clarity), rajas (activity), tamas (inertia).

label: Sattva
definition: The quality of purity, harmony, and clarity; highest guṇa.

label: Rajas
definition: The quality of activity, passion, and desire.

label: Tamas
definition: The quality of inertia, darkness, and ignorance.

label: Sannyāsa
definition: Renunciation of worldly life; 4th āśrama; path of complete detachment.

label: Viveka
definition: Discriminative wisdom; ability to distinguish the real from the unreal.

label: Vairāgya
definition: Dispassion toward worldly objects; prerequisite for Advaita practice.

label: Prasāda
definition: Divine grace; in Dvaita and Bhakti, liberation requires God's grace.
